claims.evidence_text: the drill method exhibited uttermost performance by using the seed rate 75 kg ha-1 ... the integration of the P3 (drill method) with S3 (75 kg ha-1) has potential to enhance wheat productivity
claims.claim_summary: Optimum wheat seed rate of 75 kg/ha (with drill method) maximized yield in arid Pakistan trials.

claims.evidence_text: For the drill sowing method, well-prepared soil featured lines set 22.5 cm apart.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat drill rows set 22.5 cm apart.

claims.evidence_text: the tallest being 101.67 cm under P3S3, and the shortest at 70.67 cm under P1S1 treatment
claims.claim_summary: Wheat plant height ranged 70.7-101.7 cm across seed-rate/planting-method treatments.

claims.evidence_text: P3S3 produced the highest grains spike-1, measuring 56.00 and 54.34 ... the lowest counts were observed under the P1S1 treatment, with values of 36.68 and 34.00
claims.claim_summary: Wheat grains per spike ranged 34-56 across treatments (highest with drill + 75 kg/ha).

claims.evidence_text: The highest 1000-grain weights of 47.00 g and 46.00 g were attained using the P3S3 treatment ... the lowest weights of 30.00 g and 35.00 g
claims.claim_summary: Wheat 1000-grain weight ranged 30-47 g across treatments.

claims.evidence_text: Increasing the seed rate beyond threshold led to lodging issues in wheat crop ... 75 kg ha-1 seed rate under proper plant geometry proved to be the most effective in preventing lodging
claims.claim_summary: Seed rates above ~75 kg/ha increase wheat lodging susceptibility.
